``` python
from copy import deepcopy

from fastcore.test import *
```

## JSON to XML

------------------------------------------------------------------------

<a
href="https://github.com/AnswerDotAI/toolslm/blob/main/toolslm/xml.py#L20"
target="_blank" style="float:right; font-size:smaller">source</a>

### json_to_xml

>  json_to_xml (d:dict, rnm:str)

*Convert `d` to XML.*

<table>
<thead>
<tr>
<th></th>
<th><strong>Type</strong></th>
<th><strong>Details</strong></th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td>d</td>
<td>dict</td>
<td>JSON dictionary to convert</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>rnm</td>
<td>str</td>
<td>Root name</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><strong>Returns</strong></td>
<td><strong>str</strong></td>
<td></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

JSON doesn’t map as nicely to XML as the data structure used in
`fastcore.xml`, but for simple XML trees it can be convenient – for
example:

``` python
a = dict(surname='Howard', firstnames=['Jeremy','Peter'],
         address=dict(state='Queensland',country='Australia'))
hl_md(json_to_xml(a, 'person'))
```

``` xml
<person>
  <surname>Howard</surname>
  <firstnames>
    <item>Jeremy</item>
    <item>Peter</item>
  </firstnames>
  <address>
    <state>Queensland</state>
    <country>Australia</country>
  </address>
</person>
```

## Including documents

### Notebooks

``` python
nbp = Path('00_xml.ipynb')
nb = dict2obj(nbp.read_json())
cells = nb.cells
cell = cells[7]
cell
```

``` python
{ 'cell_type': 'code',
  'execution_count': {},
  'id': '005a5be4',
  'metadata': {},
  'outputs': [],
  'source': ["a = dict(surname='Howard', firstnames=['Jeremy','Peter'],\n", "         address=dict(state='Queensland',country='Australia'))\n", "hl_md(json_to_xml(a, 'person'))"]}
```

### get_mime_text

>  get_mime_text (data)

*Get text from MIME bundle, preferring markdown over plain*

### cell2out

>  cell2out (o)

*Convert single notebook output to XML format*

``` python
for o in cell.outputs: print(to_xml(cell2out(o), do_escape=False))
```

------------------------------------------------------------------------

<a
href="https://github.com/AnswerDotAI/toolslm/blob/main/toolslm/xml.py#L53"
target="_blank" style="float:right; font-size:smaller">source</a>

### cell2xml

>  cell2xml (cell, out=True, ids=True)

*Convert notebook cell to concise XML format*

``` python
hl_md(cell2xml(cell, out=False))
```

``` xml
<code id="005a5be4">a = dict(surname='Howard', firstnames=['Jeremy','Peter'],
         address=dict(state='Queensland',country='Australia'))
hl_md(json_to_xml(a, 'person'))</code>
```

------------------------------------------------------------------------

<a
href="https://github.com/AnswerDotAI/toolslm/blob/main/toolslm/xml.py#L65"
target="_blank" style="float:right; font-size:smaller">source</a>

### nb2xml

>  nb2xml (fname=None, nb=None, out=True, ids=True)

*Convert notebook to XML format*

``` python
nbsml = deepcopy(nb)
del(nbsml.cells[2:])

hl_md(nb2xml(nb=nbsml))
```

``` xml
<notebook><code id="efe78920"><source>#|default_exp xml</code><md id="87ea05a3"><source># xml source</md></notebook>
```

``` python
hl_md(nb2xml(nb=nbsml, ids=False))
```

``` xml
<notebook><code><source>#|default_exp xml</code><md><source># xml source</md></notebook>
```

### Documents

According [to
Anthropic](https://docs.anthropic.com/claude/docs/long-context-window-tips),
“*it’s essential to structure your prompts in a way that clearly
separates the input data from the instructions*”. They recommend using
something like the following:

``` xml
Here are some documents for you to reference for your task:
    
<documents>
<document index="1">
<source>
(URL, file name, hash, etc)
</source>
<document_content>
(the text content)
</document_content>
</document>
</documents>
```

We will create some small helper functions to make it easier to generate
context in this format, although we’re use `<src>` instead of `<source>`
to avoid conflict with that HTML tag. Although it’s based on Anthropic’s
recommendation, it’s likely to work well with other models too.

We’ll use `doctype` to store our pairs.

Since Anthropic’s example shows newlines before and after each tag,
we’ll do the same.

``` python
to_xml(Src('a'))
```

    '<src>a</src>'

``` python
to_xml(Document('a'))
```

    '<document>a</document>'

------------------------------------------------------------------------

<a
href="https://github.com/AnswerDotAI/toolslm/blob/main/toolslm/xml.py#L84"
target="_blank" style="float:right; font-size:smaller">source</a>

### mk_doctype

>  mk_doctype (content:str, src:Optional[str]=None)

*Create a `doctype` named tuple*

<table>
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 6%" />
<col style="width: 25%" />
<col style="width: 34%" />
<col style="width: 34%" />
</colgroup>
<thead>
<tr>
<th></th>
<th><strong>Type</strong></th>
<th><strong>Default</strong></th>
<th><strong>Details</strong></th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td>content</td>
<td>str</td>
<td></td>
<td>The document content</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>src</td>
<td>Optional</td>
<td>None</td>
<td>URL, filename, etc; defaults to <code>md5(content)</code> if not
provided</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><strong>Returns</strong></td>
<td><strong>namedtuple</strong></td>
<td></td>
<td></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

This is a convenience wrapper to ensure that a `doctype` has the needed
information in the right format.

``` python
doc = 'This is a "sample"'
mk_doctype(doc)
```

    doctype(src='\n47e19350\n', content='\nThis is a "sample"\n')

------------------------------------------------------------------------

<a
href="https://github.com/AnswerDotAI/toolslm/blob/main/toolslm/xml.py#L92"
target="_blank" style="float:right; font-size:smaller">source</a>

### mk_doc

>  mk_doc (index:int, content:str, src:Optional[str]=None, **kwargs)

*Create an `ft` format tuple for a single doc in Anthropic’s recommended
format*

<table>
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 6%" />
<col style="width: 25%" />
<col style="width: 34%" />
<col style="width: 34%" />
</colgroup>
<thead>
<tr>
<th></th>
<th><strong>Type</strong></th>
<th><strong>Default</strong></th>
<th><strong>Details</strong></th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td>index</td>
<td>int</td>
<td></td>
<td>The document index</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>content</td>
<td>str</td>
<td></td>
<td>The document content</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>src</td>
<td>Optional</td>
<td>None</td>
<td>URL, filename, etc; defaults to <code>md5(content)</code> if not
provided</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>kwargs</td>
<td>VAR_KEYWORD</td>
<td></td>
<td></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><strong>Returns</strong></td>
<td><strong>tuple</strong></td>
<td></td>
<td></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

We can now generate XML for one document in the suggested format:

``` python
mk_doc(1, doc, title="test")
```

``` html
<document index="1" title="test"><src>
47e19350
</src><document-content>
This is a "sample"
</document-content></document>
```

------------------------------------------------------------------------

<a
href="https://github.com/AnswerDotAI/toolslm/blob/main/toolslm/xml.py#L104"
target="_blank" style="float:right; font-size:smaller">source</a>

### docs_xml

>  docs_xml (docs:list[str], srcs:Optional[list]=None, prefix:bool=False,
>                details:Optional[list]=None, title:str=None)

*Create an XML string containing `docs` in Anthropic’s recommended
format*

<table>
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 6%" />
<col style="width: 25%" />
<col style="width: 34%" />
<col style="width: 34%" />
</colgroup>
<thead>
<tr>
<th></th>
<th><strong>Type</strong></th>
<th><strong>Default</strong></th>
<th><strong>Details</strong></th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td>docs</td>
<td>list</td>
<td></td>
<td>The content of each document</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>srcs</td>
<td>Optional</td>
<td>None</td>
<td>URLs, filenames, etc; each one defaults to <code>md5(content)</code>
if not provided</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>prefix</td>
<td>bool</td>
<td>False</td>
<td>Include Anthropic’s suggested prose intro?</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>details</td>
<td>Optional</td>
<td>None</td>
<td>Optional list of dicts with additional attrs for each doc</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>title</td>
<td>str</td>
<td>None</td>
<td>Optional title attr for Documents element</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><strong>Returns</strong></td>
<td><strong>str</strong></td>
<td></td>
<td></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

Putting it all together, we have our final XML format:

``` python
docs = [doc, 'And another one']
srcs = [None, 'doc.txt']
print(docs_xml(docs, srcs))
```

    <documents><document index="1"><src>
    47e19350
    </src><document-content>
    This is a "sample"
    </document-content></document><document index="2"><src>
    doc.txt
    </src><document-content>
    And another one
    </document-content></document></documents>

## Context creation

Now that we can generate Anthropic’s XML format, let’s make it easy for
a few common cases.

### File list to context

For generating XML context from files, we’ll just read them as text and
use the file names as `src`.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

<a
href="https://github.com/AnswerDotAI/toolslm/blob/main/toolslm/xml.py#L119"
target="_blank" style="float:right; font-size:smaller">source</a>

### read_file

>  read_file (fname, out=True, max_size=None, ids=True)

*Read file content, converting notebooks to XML if needed*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

<a
href="https://github.com/AnswerDotAI/toolslm/blob/main/toolslm/xml.py#L129"
target="_blank" style="float:right; font-size:smaller">source</a>

### files2ctx

>  files2ctx (fnames:list[typing.Union[str,pathlib.Path]], out:bool=True,
>                 srcs:Optional[list]=None, max_size:int=None, ids:bool=True,
>                 prefix:bool=False, details:Optional[list]=None,
>                 title:str=None)

*Convert files to XML context, handling notebooks*

<table>
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 6%" />
<col style="width: 25%" />
<col style="width: 34%" />
<col style="width: 34%" />
</colgroup>
<thead>
<tr>
<th></th>
<th><strong>Type</strong></th>
<th><strong>Default</strong></th>
<th><strong>Details</strong></th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td>fnames</td>
<td>list</td>
<td></td>
<td>List of file names to add to context</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>out</td>
<td>bool</td>
<td>True</td>
<td>Include notebook cell outputs?</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>srcs</td>
<td>Optional</td>
<td>None</td>
<td>Use the labels instead of <code>fnames</code></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>max_size</td>
<td>int</td>
<td>None</td>
<td>Skip files larger than this (bytes)</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>ids</td>
<td>bool</td>
<td>True</td>
<td>Include cell ids in notebooks?</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>prefix</td>
<td>bool</td>
<td>False</td>
<td>Include Anthropic’s suggested prose intro?</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>details</td>
<td>Optional</td>
<td>None</td>
<td>Optional list of dicts with additional attrs for each doc</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>title</td>
<td>str</td>
<td>None</td>
<td>Optional title attr for Documents element</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><strong>Returns</strong></td>
<td><strong>str</strong></td>
<td></td>
<td><strong>XML for LM context</strong></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

``` python
fnames = ['samples/sample_core.py', 'samples/sample_styles.css']
hl_md(files2ctx(fnames, max_size=120))
```

``` xml
<documents><document index="1"><src>
samples/sample_core.py
</src><document-content>
[Skipped: sample_core.py exceeds 120 bytes]
</document-content></document><document index="2"><src>
samples/sample_styles.css
</src><document-content>
.cell { margin-bottom: 1rem; }
.cell > .sourceCode { margin-bottom: 0; }
.cell-output > pre { margin-bottom: 0; }
</document-content></document></documents>
```

### folder2ctx

>  folder2ctx (folder:Union[str,pathlib.Path], prefix:bool=False,
>                  out:bool=True, include_base:bool=True, title:str=None,
>                  max_size:int=100000, max_total:int=10000000,
>                  readme_first:bool=False, files_only:bool=False,
>                  ids:bool=True, path:Path|str='.', recursive:bool=True,
>                  symlinks:bool=True, file_glob:str=None, file_re:str=None,

>                  skip_file_re:str=None, skip_folder_re:str=None,
>                  func:callable=<function join>, ret_folders:bool=False,
>                  sort:bool=True, types:str|list=None, exts:str|list=None)

*Convert folder contents to XML context, handling notebooks*

<colgroup>

<col style="width: 25%" />
<col style="width: 34%" />
<col style="width: 34%" />
</colgroup>

<th><strong>Details</strong></th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td>folder</td>
<td>Union</td>
<td></td>
<td>Folder to read</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>prefix</td>
<td>bool</td>
<td>False</td>
<td>Include Anthropic’s suggested prose intro?</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>out</td>
<td>bool</td>
<td>True</td>
<td>Include notebook cell outputs?</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>include_base</td>
<td>bool</td>
<td>True</td>
<td>Include full path in src?</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>title</td>
<td>str</td>
<td>None</td>
<td>Optional title attr for Documents element</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>max_size</td>
<td>int</td>
<td>100000</td>
<td>Skip files larger than this (bytes)</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>max_total</td>
<td>int</td>
<td>10000000</td>
<td>Max total output size in bytes</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>readme_first</td>
<td>bool</td>
<td>False</td>
<td>Prioritize README files at start of context?</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>files_only</td>
<td>bool</td>
<td>False</td>
<td>Return dict of {filename: size} instead of context?</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>ids</td>
<td>bool</td>
<td>True</td>
<td>Include cell ids in notebooks?</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>path</td>
<td>pathlib.Path | str</td>
<td>.</td>
<td>path to start searching</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>recursive</td>
<td>bool</td>
<td>True</td>
<td>search subfolders</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>symlinks</td>
<td>bool</td>
<td>True</td>
<td>follow symlinks?</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>file_glob</td>
<td>str</td>
<td>None</td>
<td>Only include files matching glob</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>file_re</td>
<td>str</td>
<td>None</td>
<td>Only include files matching regex</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>folder_re</td>
<td>str</td>
<td>None</td>
<td>Only enter folders matching regex</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>skip_file_glob</td>
<td>str</td>
<td>None</td>
<td>Skip files matching glob</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>skip_file_re</td>
<td>str</td>
<td>None</td>
<td>Skip files matching regex</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>skip_folder_re</td>
<td>str</td>
<td>None</td>
<td>Skip folders matching regex,</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>func</td>
<td>callable</td>
<td>join</td>
<td>function to apply to each matched file</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>ret_folders</td>
<td>bool</td>
<td>False</td>
<td>return folders, not just files</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>sort</td>
<td>bool</td>
<td>True</td>
<td>sort files by name within each folder</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>types</td>
<td>str | list</td>
<td>None</td>
<td>list or comma-separated str of ext types from: py, js, java, c, cpp,
rb, r, ex, sh, web, doc, cfg</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>exts</td>
<td>str | list</td>
<td>None</td>
<td>list or comma-separated str of exts to include</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><strong>Returns</strong></td>
<td><strong>L</strong></td>
<td></td>
<td><strong>Paths to matched files</strong></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

``` python
print(folder2ctx('samples', prefix=True, types='py'))
```

    Here are some documents for you to reference for your task:

    <documents><document index="1"><src>
    samples/sample_core.py
    </src><document-content>
    import inspect
    empty = inspect.Parameter.empty
    models = 'claude-3-opus-20240229','claude-3-sonnet-20240229','claude-3-haiku-20240307'
    </document-content></document></documents>

<td>Only include files matching glob</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>file_re</td>
<td>str</td>
<td>None</td>
<td>Only include files matching regex</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>folder_re</td>
<td>str</td>
<td>None</td>
<td>Only enter folders matching regex</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>skip_file_glob</td>
<td>str</td>
<td>None</td>
<td>Skip files matching glob</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>skip_folder_re</td>
<td>str</td>
<td>None</td>
<td>Skip folders matching regex,</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>func</td>
<td>callable</td>
<td>join</td>
<td>function to apply to each matched file</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>ret_folders</td>
<td>bool</td>
<td>False</td>
<td>return folders, not just files</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>sort</td>
<td>bool</td>
<td>True</td>
<td>sort files by name within each folder</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>exts</td>
<td>str | list</td>
<td>None</td>
<td>list or comma-separated str of exts to include</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><strong>Returns</strong></td>
<td><strong>L</strong></td>
<td></td>
<td><strong>Paths to matched files</strong></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

<div>

> **Tip**
>
> After you install `toolslm`,
> [`folder2ctx`](https://AnswerDotAI.github.io/toolslm/xml.html#folder2ctx)
> becomes available from the command line.

</div>

``` python
!folder2ctx -h
```

    usage: folder2ctx [-h] [--path PATH] [--recursive] [--symlinks]
                      [--file_glob FILE_GLOB] [--file_re FILE_RE]
                      [--folder_re FOLDER_RE] [--skip_file_glob SKIP_FILE_GLOB]
                      [--skip_file_re SKIP_FILE_RE]
                      [--skip_folder_re SKIP_FOLDER_RE] [--func FUNC]
                      [--ret_folders] [--sort] [--types TYPES] [--exts EXTS]
                      [--prefix] [--out] [--include_base] [--title TITLE]
                      [--max_size MAX_SIZE] [--max_total MAX_TOTAL] [--readme_first]
                      [--files_only]
                      folder

    CLI to convert folder contents to XML context, handling notebooks

    positional arguments:
      folder                           Folder name containing files to add to
                                       context

    options:
      -h, --help                       show this help message and exit
      --path PATH                      path to start searching (default: .)
      --recursive                      search subfolders (default: False)
      --symlinks                       follow symlinks? (default: False)
      --file_glob FILE_GLOB            Only include files matching glob
      --file_re FILE_RE                Only include files matching regex
      --folder_re FOLDER_RE            Only enter folders matching regex
      --skip_file_glob SKIP_FILE_GLOB  Skip files matching glob
      --skip_file_re SKIP_FILE_RE      Skip files matching regex
      --skip_folder_re SKIP_FOLDER_RE  Skip folders matching regex,
      --func FUNC                      function to apply to each matched file
                                       (default: <function join>)
      --ret_folders                    return folders, not just files (default:
                                       False)
      --sort                           sort files by name within each folder
                                       (default: False)
      --types TYPES                    list or comma-separated str of ext types
                                       from: py, js, java, c, cpp, rb, r, ex, sh,
                                       web, doc, cfg
      --exts EXTS                      list or comma-separated str of exts to
                                       include
      --prefix                         Include Anthropic's suggested prose intro?
                                       (default: False)
      --out                            Include notebook cell outputs? (default:
                                       False)
      --include_base                   Include full path in src? (default: False)
      --title TITLE                    Optional title attr for Documents element
      --max_size MAX_SIZE              Skip files larger than this (bytes) (default:
                                       100000)
      --max_total MAX_TOTAL            Max total output size in bytes (default:
                                       10000000)
      --readme_first                   Prioritize README files at start of context?
                                       (default: False)
      --files_only                     Return dict of {filename: size} instead of
                                       context? (default: False)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

href="https://github.com/AnswerDotAI/toolslm/blob/main/toolslm/xml.py#L208"
target="_blank" style="float:right; font-size:smaller">source</a>

*Parse GitHub URL into (owner, repo, type, ref, path) or None*

<a
href="https://github.com/AnswerDotAI/toolslm/blob/main/toolslm/xml.py#L215"
target="_blank" style="float:right; font-size:smaller">source</a>

### repo2ctx

>  repo2ctx (owner:str, repo:str=None, ref:str=None, folder:str=None,
>                show_filters:bool=True, token:str=None, prefix:bool=False,
>                out:bool=True, include_base:bool=True, title:str=None,
>                max_size:int=100000, max_total:int=10000000,
>                readme_first:bool=False, files_only:bool=False, ids:bool=True,
>                path:Path|str='.', recursive:bool=True, symlinks:bool=True,
>                file_glob:str=None, file_re:str=None, folder_re:str=None,
>                skip_file_glob:str=None, skip_file_re:str=None,
>                skip_folder_re:str=None, func:callable=<function join>,
>                ret_folders:bool=False, sort:bool=True, types:str|list=None,
>                exts:str|list=None)

*Convert GitHub repo to XML context without cloning*

<table>
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 6%" />
<col style="width: 25%" />
<col style="width: 34%" />
<col style="width: 34%" />
</colgroup>
<thead>
<tr>
<th></th>
<th><strong>Type</strong></th>
<th><strong>Default</strong></th>
<th><strong>Details</strong></th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td>owner</td>
<td>str</td>
<td></td>
<td>GitHub repo owner or “owner/repo” or a full github URL</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>repo</td>
<td>str</td>
<td>None</td>
<td>GitHub repo name (leave empty if using “owner/repo” or URL format
for owner param)</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>ref</td>
<td>str</td>
<td>None</td>
<td>Git ref (branch/tag/sha) (get from URL not provided); defaults to
repo’s default branch</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>folder</td>
<td>str</td>
<td>None</td>
<td>Only include files under this path (get from URL not provided)</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>show_filters</td>
<td>bool</td>
<td>True</td>
<td>Include filter info in title?</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>token</td>
<td>str</td>
<td>None</td>
<td>GitHub token (uses GITHUB_TOKEN env var if None)</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>prefix</td>
<td>bool</td>
<td>False</td>
<td>Include Anthropic’s suggested prose intro?</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>out</td>
<td>bool</td>
<td>True</td>
<td>Include notebook cell outputs?</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>include_base</td>
<td>bool</td>
<td>True</td>
<td>Include full path in src?</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>title</td>
<td>str</td>
<td>None</td>
<td>Optional title attr for Documents element</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>max_size</td>
<td>int</td>
<td>100000</td>
<td>Skip files larger than this (bytes)</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>max_total</td>
<td>int</td>
<td>10000000</td>
<td>Max total output size in bytes</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>readme_first</td>
<td>bool</td>
<td>False</td>
<td>Prioritize README files at start of context?</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>files_only</td>
<td>bool</td>
<td>False</td>
<td>Return dict of {filename: size} instead of context?</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>ids</td>
<td>bool</td>
<td>True</td>
<td>Include cell ids in notebooks?</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>path</td>
<td>pathlib.Path | str</td>
<td>.</td>
<td>path to start searching</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>recursive</td>
<td>bool</td>
<td>True</td>
<td>search subfolders</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>symlinks</td>
<td>bool</td>
<td>True</td>
<td>follow symlinks?</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>file_glob</td>
<td>str</td>
<td>None</td>
<td>Only include files matching glob</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>file_re</td>
<td>str</td>
<td>None</td>
<td>Only include files matching regex</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>folder_re</td>
<td>str</td>
<td>None</td>
<td>Only enter folders matching regex</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>skip_file_glob</td>
<td>str</td>
<td>None</td>
<td>Skip files matching glob</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>skip_file_re</td>
<td>str</td>
<td>None</td>
<td>Skip files matching regex</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>skip_folder_re</td>
<td>str</td>
<td>None</td>
<td>Skip folders matching regex,</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>func</td>
<td>callable</td>
<td>join</td>
<td>function to apply to each matched file</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>ret_folders</td>
<td>bool</td>
<td>False</td>
<td>return folders, not just files</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>sort</td>
<td>bool</td>
<td>True</td>
<td>sort files by name within each folder</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>types</td>
<td>str | list</td>
<td>None</td>
<td>list or comma-separated str of ext types from: py, js, java, c, cpp,
rb, r, ex, sh, web, doc, cfg</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>exts</td>
<td>str | list</td>
<td>None</td>
<td>list or comma-separated str of exts to include</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><strong>Returns</strong></td>
<td><strong>Union</strong></td>
<td></td>
<td><strong>XML for LM context, or dict of file sizes</strong></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

``` python
print(repo2ctx('answerdotai/toolslm', exts=('ipynb','py'), skip_file_re='^_', out=False, max_total=500))
```
